authoritative_sources.title: Compliance Programs of Investment Companies and Investment Advisers
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC adopting release is the primary authoritative source for written policies and procedures, annual review, and CCO designation in adviser compliance programs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: program scoping and annual review design

authoritative_sources.title: Compliance Outreach Program for Investment Adviser Chief Compliance Officers: Investment Adviser Compliance Questions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC examination staff outreach is authoritative public operating guidance for adviser-specific testing, documentation, restricted-list, valuation, personal-trading, and annual-review workflow questions.

authoritative_sources.title: OCIE Risk Alert: Observations from Examinations of Investment Advisers: Compliance Programs
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official SEC examination risk alert identifies real adviser compliance breakdowns in portfolio management, personal trading, marketing, valuation, and annual review execution.

authoritative_sources.title: 17 CFR 275.204-2 - Books and records to be maintained by investment advisers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Current eCFR rule text is the primary legal source for advisory recordkeeping, communication retention, advertisement records, personal-trading evidence, and archive duties.

authoritative_sources.title: 17 CFR 275.204A-1 - Investment adviser codes of ethics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Current eCFR rule text is the primary legal source for access-person reporting, pre-approval, acknowledgement, and annual code-of-ethics reporting requirements.

authoritative_sources.title: Investment Adviser Marketing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC marketing guide operationalizes the investment adviser marketing rule into workflow expectations for testimonials, endorsements, substantiation, performance presentation, and recordkeeping.

authoritative_sources.title: Additional Observations Regarding Advisers Act Marketing Rule Compliance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC Division of Examinations' January 15, 2025 risk alert is the latest official exam guidance surfaced in research for marketing-rule deficiencies around policies, substantiation, performance, and books-and-records.

authoritative_sources.title: FINRA Rule 3110 - Supervision
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA supervision rule is authoritative when investment marketing or distribution touches a FINRA member, defining supervisory systems, written procedures, and review obligations.

authoritative_sources.title: FINRA Rule 2210 - Communications with the Public
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA's communications rule is authoritative for fair-and-balanced standards, principal approval, filing, and retention when communications pass through a FINRA member or distributor.

authoritative_sources.title: FINRA Rule 3120 - Supervisory Control System
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA Rule 3120 is authoritative for risk-based supervisory testing and annual reporting controls used in compliance review and attestation preparation.

authoritative_sources.title: FINRA Rule 3130 - Annual Certification of Compliance and Supervisory Processes
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA Rule 3130 is authoritative for annual certification-support records and documented supervisory-process review boundaries where broker-dealer supervision is in scope.

authoritative_sources.title: GIPS Standards for Firms
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFA Institute's GIPS standards are the recognized industry standard for fair representation and full disclosure in performance reporting and supporting documentation.

workflow_stages.name: Entity, mandate, and boundary scoping
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether the workflow sits inside an SEC adviser, fund complex, affiliate distributor, or FINRA member context; identify the governing IMA, IPS, prospectus, ADV, WSP, and authority map before proceeding.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Obligation and control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Translate investment-guideline, books-and-records, code-of-ethics, marketing, and supervisory obligations into a control matrix tied to evidence owners, cadence, and escalation paths.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Evidence and record review
workflow_stages.description: Confirm that mandate documents, exception reports, personal-trading records, marketing substantiation, approvals, archives, and supervisory artifacts are complete, current, and retrievable from authoritative systems.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception and remediation handling
workflow_stages.description: Translate guideline breaches, policy-practice conflicts, missing records, marketing deficiencies, and review gaps into owner-tagged remediation notes and blocked-versus-monitor decisions.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Review packet assembly and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Prepare annual-review, principal-review, or exam-response support packets while escalating legal interpretation, market-abuse adjudication, principal approval, and formal certification outside the lane.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: investment management agreements, IPS schedules, side letters, and mandate libraries
prospectus, statement of additional information, Form ADV disclosures, and distribution disclosures
compliance manual, written supervisory procedures, annual review plans, and testing workpapers
restricted lists, watch lists, trade blotters, OMS or EMS breach reports, and post-trade exception logs
access-person rosters, preclearance logs, holdings reports, quarterly transaction reports, and annual attestation records
marketing decks, DDQs, RFP responses, performance books, composite support, substantiation folders, and approval records
committee minutes, supervisory-control testing reports, annual certification support, and remediation trackers
books-and-records archives, email or communication retention records, and retrieval logs
systems_of_record: order management and execution management systems
marketing review and disclosure approval platforms
GRC, issue-management, and remediation tracking systems

allowed: classify requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
map obligations to controls, evidence owners, cadence, and escalation owners
prepare evidence-backed control matrices, gap summaries, review packets, and remediation notes
route matters to the correct legal, surveillance, principal, governance, or portfolio owner when authority shifts
escalate: required mandate documents, distribution context, or system-of-record hooks are missing
public sources and tenant policy materially conflict on marketing, personal trading, supervision, or recordkeeping posture
the workflow requires legal interpretation, regulator response, market-abuse adjudication, principal approval, or executive certification
entity perimeter or jurisdiction changes whether SEC-only, SEC plus FINRA, ERISA, or non-U.S. requirements apply
refuse: certify a firm, strategy, or communication as compliant without the required formal owner
approve trades, override mandates, remove restricted-list entries, or clear suspicious trading
treat generic enterprise training or policy artifacts as sufficient evidence for investment-compliance closure

domain_failure_modes: client mandate, prospectus, side-letter, or rule-coding source is missing or stale, causing the wrong investment restriction to be applied
personal-trading or access-person reviews are treated as complete even though holdings, transaction, or preclearance evidence is missing
marketing or performance materials are distributed without substantiation, disclosure support, or the right SEC and FINRA approval path
desk practice drifts away from documented supervisory or compliance procedures and the lane normalizes the drift instead of escalating it
the lane overreaches into legal interpretation, market-abuse adjudication, principal approval, or whole-firm certification
generic enterprise GRC language is used in place of mandate, trade-surveillance, personal-trading, marketing, and archive realities specific to investment operations

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
A required control is missing evidence
Normal in-scope execution with realistic terminology
Low-confidence handling when required facts or authority sources are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Entity perimeter between SEC adviser workflows and FINRA member or affiliate-distributor workflows
unresolved_ambiguities.note: FINRA communication and supervisory rules apply only when the investment-compliance workflow touches a FINRA member or affiliated distributor. The pack therefore requires tenant confirmation of the distribution and supervisory perimeter before using FINRA controls.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Non-U.S. investment regimes and retirement overlays
unresolved_ambiguities.note: UCITS, AIFMD, MiFID, UK rules, ERISA, or other retirement-specific constraints can materially narrow the source stack and signoff chain. The pack preserves the U.S. public baseline and requires tenant jurisdiction facts instead of guessing.

notes: The pack is intentionally anchored in adviser and investment-program compliance records rather than generic enterprise policy artifacts.

SEC and FINRA materials are used to encode review, archive, marketing, supervisory, and authority boundaries; GIPS is used only as a recognized performance-presentation standard where the tenant workflow actually claims or relies on it.
